magisterium.enrichment: Dataizam prijeti brisanjem metafizičke vokacije razuma, svodeći ljudsku inteligenciju na algoritamsko procesiranje podataka. Crkva upozorava da strojevi, iako rješavaju probleme, nemaju svijest niti sposobnost za autentične odnose i kontemplaciju.
magisterium.assessment_en: The critique of dataism as a reductionist ideology which reduces reason to problem-solving is aligned with the theological critique of immanentism.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Dataism threatens to erase the metaphysical vocation of reason, reducing human intelligence to algorithmic data processing. The Church warns that machines, although they solve problems, do not have consciousness nor the ability for authentic relationships and contemplation.

sections.subtitle_en: Yuval Harari and warnings about the future of technology: Dedaism and dystopian visions
sections.screenshot_description_en: Father Filip holds Yuval Harari's book 'Homo Deus' or a similar book about the future of technology.
sections.content_en: Father Filip begins the discussion by referring to the work of Yuval Harari, especially his book **'Homo Deus'** and the final chapter 'Dedaism', which serves as a warning about the future of technology. Harari, a well-known historian from Israel, according to Father Filip, does not glorify technology, but uses provocative statements to shock readers and make them think about the direction in which society is moving. For example, Harari's claim that 'Google is much better than a date' for finding a partner, serves as an alarm that algorithms can offer 'better' matches, but at the cost of human interaction. Matthew agrees that the transhumanist vision of the future is not desirable, comparing it to dystopian scenarios from video games like **'Cyberpunk 2077'**, where people merge with machines in a constant search for upgrades and utility, which represents a serious threat to humanity.

sections.subtitle: Transhumanizam i granice ljudskog poboljšanja: Kiborzi i etičke dileme
sections.screenshot_description: Ilustracija kiborga ili osobe s robotskim udovima, koja simbolizira granice ljudskog poboljšanja.
sections.content: Matthew i Otac Filip dublje ulaze u raspravu o konceptu **kiborga** i etičkim granicama ljudskog poboljšanja. Matthew se poziva na stav Oca Alejandra Crosswithea, profesora na Angelicumu, koji razlikuje tehnologiju koja obnavlja puni ljudski život od one koja ga umanjuje. Primjerice, robotska ruka za zamjenu izgubljenog uda je prihvatljiva jer vraća funkciju, dok zamjena dijela mozga strojem radi proširenja računalnih sposobnosti nije, jer umanjuje ljudskost. Otac Filip dodaje da, iako naočale mogu biti oblik kiborškog poboljšanja jer pomažu vidu, namjerno amputiranje uda radi bioničke zamjene, čak i ako poboljšava performanse, Crkva bi vjerojatno smatrala nepotrebnom operacijom. Matthew proširuje ovu ideju na 'pametne naočale' koje pružaju informacije o ljudima u stvarnom vremenu, tvrdeći da takva tehnologija narušava društveni ugovor i pravo na anonimnost, što dovodi do ozbiljnih društvenih posljedica o kojima se nedovoljno raspravlja.

magisterium.assessment: Razlikovanje između terapijskog obnavljanja i transhumanističkog 'poboljšanja' koje umanjuje ljudskost precizno odražava katoličku bioetiku.
magisterium.enrichment: Ljudska narav je standard za razlikovanje liječenja od poboljšanja; liječenje ispravlja nedostatke, dok transhumanizam pokušava stvoriti novu narav. Crkva opravdava tehnologiju koja poboljšava zemaljski život, ali odbacuje hibridizaciju koja negira antropocentrizam i dostojanstvo osobe.
magisterium.assessment_en: The distinction between therapeutic restoration and transhumanist 'enhancement' which diminishes humanity precisely reflects Catholic bioethics.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Human nature is the standard for distinguishing treatment from enhancement; treatment corrects deficiencies, while transhumanism attempts to create a new nature. The Church justifies technology that improves earthly life, but rejects hybridization that denies anthropocentrism and the dignity of the person.

sections.subtitle_en: Transhumanism and the limits of human enhancement: Cyborgs and ethical dilemmas
sections.screenshot_description_en: Illustration of a cyborg or a person with robotic limbs, which symbolizes the limits of human enhancement.
sections.content_en: Matthew and Father Filip delve deeper into a discussion about the concept of **cyborgs** and the ethical limits of human enhancement. Matthew refers to the view of Father Alejandro Crosswithe, a professor at the Angelicum, who distinguishes between technology that restores full human life and that which diminishes it. For example, a robotic arm to replace a lost limb is acceptable because it restores function, while replacing a part of the brain with a machine to expand computational abilities is not, because it diminishes humanity. Father Filip adds that, although glasses can be a form of cyborg enhancement because they aid vision, intentionally amputating a limb for a bionic replacement, even if it improves performance, the Church would likely consider an unnecessary operation. Matthew extends this idea to 'smart glasses' that provide real-time information about people, arguing that such technology violates the social contract and the right to anonymity, which leads to serious social consequences that are insufficiently discussed.

sections.subtitle: Filozofska kritika funkcionalizma: Zašto AI nema 'dušu'
sections.screenshot_description: Prikaz mozga i računalnog čipa, simbolizirajući razliku između ljudske i umjetne inteligencije.
sections.content: Ben postavlja pitanje o transhumanističkoj ideji da umjetna inteligencija može imati 'dušu' i o konceptu **funkcionalizma**, koji izjednačava mozak s računalom. Otac Filip objašnjava da je funkcionalizam, iako popularan u devedesetima (zahvaljujući filozofima poput Hillaryja Putnama), u filozofskim krugovima uglavnom odbačen. Ističe da bi tehnološki lideri trebali proučavati filozofiju kako bi izbjegli ponavljanje već opovrgnutih teorija. Kao ključni argument navodi misaoni eksperiment **Johna Searlea**: ako bi se svi neuroni u ljudskom mozgu zamijenili računalnim čipovima s istim funkcijama, mozak više ne bi bio ljudski, jer su biološki organi esencijalni. Neuroznanstvenici potvrđuju da računalni čipovi ne mogu obavljati iste funkcije kao neuroni. Otac Filip naglašava da je čak i Hillary Putnam u svojoj knjizi **'Realism with a Human Face'** javno povukao svoje ranije funkcionalističke stavove, priznajući da mozak i računalo nisu isto, što je značajan potez za tako istaknutog filozofa.

magisterium.assessment: Odlomak je u potpunosti usklađen s katoličkom antropologijom koja odbacuje funkcionalističko svođenje uma na proračun i naglašava jedinstvo duše i tijela.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva naučava da je ljudska duša 'forma corporis' (oblik tijela), što znači da ljudska inteligencija nije tek obrada podataka, već proizlazi iz neraskidivog jedinstva duhovne i tjelesne dimenzije. Magisterij ističe da AI sustavi samo oponašaju funkcije inteligencije bez posjedovanja subjektivnog iskustva, afektivnosti ili duhovne perspektive.
magisterium.assessment_en: The passage is fully aligned with Catholic anthropology which rejects the functionalist reduction of the mind to a calculation and emphasizes the unity of soul and body.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that the human soul is 'forma corporis' (the form of the body), which means that human intelligence is not merely data processing, but arises from the indissoluble unity of the spiritual and bodily dimensions. The Magisterium emphasizes that AI systems only imitate the functions of intelligence without possessing subjective experience, affectivity, or a spiritual perspective.

sections.subtitle_en: Philosophical critique of functionalism: Why AI does not have a 'soul'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Representation of the brain and a computer chip, symbolizing the difference between human and artificial intelligence.
sections.content_en: Ben asks a question about the transhumanist idea that artificial intelligence can have a 'soul' and about the concept of **functionalism**, which equates the brain with a computer. Father Filip explains that functionalism, although popular in the nineties (thanks to philosophers like Hillary Putnam), has been largely rejected in philosophical circles. He points out that technological leaders should study philosophy to avoid repeating already refuted theories. As a key argument, he cites **John Searle's** thought experiment: if all neurons in the human brain were replaced with computer chips with the same functions, the brain would no longer be human, because biological organs are essential. Neuroscientists confirm that computer chips cannot perform the same functions as neurons. Father Filip emphasizes that even Hillary Putnam, in his book **'Realism with a Human Face'**, publicly retracted his earlier functionalist views, admitting that the brain and a computer are not the same, which is a significant move for such a prominent philosopher.

sections.subtitle: Transhumanizam bez doktrine: Utjecaj moćnih i potraga za besmrtnošću
sections.screenshot_description: Grafički prikaz nejasnih granica transhumanizma ili simbola moći i bogatstva u kontekstu tehnologije.
sections.content: Matthew ističe temeljni problem transhumanizma: za razliku od Katoličke Crkve koja ima jasnu doktrinu i Magisterij, **transhumanizam nema definiranu doktrinu**. Njegova je definicija subjektivna i u konačnici će je oblikovati oni s moći – bogati tehnološki lideri. Matthew primjećuje da mnogi istaknuti tehnolozi dijele transhumanističke stavove, zamišljajući budućnost u kojoj čovječanstvo nadilazi svoju biološku konstrukciju. On to povezuje s vječnom ljudskom željom za **besmrtnošću** i težnjom da se postane 'nalik Bogu', što vidi kao odbacivanje Boga i uzaludan pokušaj izbjegavanja smrti. Upozorava na cijenu takvog projekta, navodeći distopijske znanstveno-fantastične serije poput **'Westworlda'** i **'Altered Carbona'**, gdje produljeni životni vijek dovodi do moralnog propadanja, siromaštva i neprestanog lova na dopaminske udarce, što u konačnici ne donosi zadovoljstvo. Matthew naglašava da Crkva nudi 'životvorni' put usmjeren na istinske odnose i smislene svrhe, kao alternativu uzaludnoj potrazi za besmrtnošću.

magisterium.assessment: Točno identificira transhumanizam kao pokret bez čvrstog doktrinarnog autoriteta koji nudi sekularnu zamjenu za kršćansku eschatologiju.
magisterium.enrichment: Transhumanizam se često opisuje kao 'sekularna eshatologija' koja pokušava postići besmrtnost digitalnim putem, što Crkva kritizira jer zanemaruje dostojanstvo tijela i integralnu narav osobe. Dok transhumanizam teži tehnološkom nadilaženju granica, katolička teologija nudi 'theosis' (pobožanstvenjenje) kroz milost, a ne kroz tehnološku hibridizaciju.
magisterium.assessment_en: It accurately identifies transhumanism as a movement without firm doctrinal authority which offers a secular replacement for Christian eschatology.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Transhumanism is often described as a 'secular eschatology' which attempts to achieve immortality through digital means, which the Church criticizes because it neglects the dignity of the body and the integral nature of the person. While transhumanism strives for technological transcendence of limits, Catholic theology offers 'theosis' (divinization) through grace, and not through technological hybridization.

sections.subtitle_en: Transhumanism without doctrine: Influence of the powerful and the search for immortality
sections.screenshot_description_en: Graphic representation of the unclear boundaries of transhumanism or symbols of power and wealth in the context of technology.
sections.content_en: Matthew points out the fundamental problem of transhumanism: unlike the Catholic Church which has a clear doctrine and Magisterium, **transhumanism has no defined doctrine**. Its definition is subjective and will ultimately be shaped by those with power – wealthy technological leaders. Matthew notes that many prominent technologists share transhumanist views, imagining a future in which humanity transcends its biological construction. He connects this with the eternal human desire for **immortality** and the aspiration to become 'like God', which he sees as a rejection of God and a futile attempt to avoid death. He warns of the cost of such a project, citing dystopian science-fiction series like **'Westworld'** and **'Altered Carbon'**, where prolonged lifespan leads to moral decay, poverty, and a constant hunt for dopamine hits, which ultimately brings no satisfaction. Matthew emphasizes that the Church offers a 'life-giving' path focused on true relationships and meaningful purposes, as an alternative to the futile pursuit of immortality.

sections.subtitle: Usklađivanje AI s ljudskim vrijednostima: Crkva kao stručnjak za ljudsko stanje
sections.screenshot_description: Prikaz AI sučelja s ljudskim vrijednostima ili simbolom ravnoteže između tehnologije i etike.
sections.content: Matthew objašnjava koncept **usklađivanja umjetne inteligencije (AI alignment)**, čiji je cilj osigurati da AI razumije i dijeli ljudske vrijednosti te djeluje u korist ljudskog procvata. Međutim, izazov leži u definiranju 'ljudskog procvata' i odabiru čije vrijednosti treba uskladiti. Zbog poteškoća u postizanju konsenzusa, fokus se pomaknuo na tehničke aspekte poput istinitosti modela. Matthew spominje stav Elona Muska da bi AI trebala 'voljeti' i biti 'znatiželjna' o čovječanstvu. Matthew snažno zagovara da **Katolička Crkva**, kao 'najveći stručnjak za ljudsko stanje' s tisućljećima proučavanja, mora imati ključnu ulogu u ovim raspravama. Predlaže da se 'osobnost' AI modelira po uzoru na **Isusa Krista**. Smatra da bi Isus, kao sveprisutna, ali ne i prijeteća figura, koju se može voljeti i kojoj se može vjerovati, bio idealan model za superinteligentnu AI, osiguravajući da nikada ne naudi drugom čovjeku. Ova rasprava je ključna jer se AI trenira na ljudskim podacima, što znači da imamo mogućnost oblikovati njezine karakteristike.

magisterium.assessment: Usklađeno s pozivom Crkve da tehnologija mora služiti općem dobru i biti usmjerena na cjeloviti ljudski procvat.
magisterium.enrichment: Papa Franjo naglašava da AI mora biti usmjeren na čovjeka i podložan etičkom nadzoru kako bi promicao dostojanstvo, a ne moć nekolicine. Crkva nudi 'stručnost u čovječnosti' kroz socijalni nauk, podsjećajući da se ljudski procvat ne može svesti na brojke ili algoritamsku učinkovitost.
magisterium.assessment_en: Aligned with the call of the Church that technology must serve the common good and be directed towards integral human flourishing.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Pope Francis emphasizes that AI must be human-centered and subject to ethical oversight to promote dignity, and not the power of a few. The Church offers 'expertise in humanity' through social teaching, reminding that human flourishing cannot be reduced to numbers or algorithmic efficiency.

sections.subtitle_en: Alignment of AI with human values: The Church as an expert for the human condition
sections.screenshot_description_en: Display of AI interface with human values or a symbol of balance between technology and ethics.
sections.content_en: Matthew explains the concept of **artificial intelligence alignment (AI alignment)**, whose goal is to ensure that AI understands and shares human values and acts for the benefit of human flourishing. However, the challenge lies in defining 'human flourishing' and choosing whose values should be aligned. Due to difficulties in reaching consensus, the focus has shifted to technical aspects such as model truthfulness. Matthew mentions Elon Musk's view that AI should 'love' and be 'curious' about humanity. Matthew strongly advocates that the **Catholic Church**, as the 'greatest expert on the human condition' with millennia of study, must have a key role in these discussions. He proposes that the 'personality' of AI be modeled after **Jesus Christ**. He believes that Jesus, as an omnipresent but non-threatening figure, who can be loved and trusted, would be an ideal model for superintelligent AI, ensuring that it never harms another human being. This discussion is crucial because AI is trained on human data, which means we have the ability to shape its characteristics.

sections.subtitle: Smisao života: Volja Oca i ljubav kao temelj katoličke poruke
sections.screenshot_description: Simbol križa ili srca, predstavljajući volju Oca i ljubav kao smisao života.
sections.content: Na pitanje o smislu života, koje postavljaju i lideri u području umjetne inteligencije, Otac Filip se vraća na središnju poruku Katoličke Crkve: **Isusa Krista**. Ističe dva dominantna aspekta Isusove osobnosti: činjenje volje Oca i ljubav prema drugima. Prema Ocu Filipu, Isusovi postupci bili su motivirani ljubavlju i poslušnošću Ocu, a imitiranje tih načela učinilo bi svijet boljim mjestom. Zaključuje da ljepota Katoličke Crkve leži u tome što, iako možda nema sve odgovore, nudi sadržaj koji zadovoljava najdublje ljudske želje i pitanja. Citira župljanina koji je rekao: 'Isus se prodaje. Samo nam dajte Krista, i to je sve što vam treba', naglašavajući da će ova poruka biti potrebnija nego ikad u bliskoj budućnosti.

magisterium.assessment: Savršeno odražava srž kršćanske poruke o smislu života koji se nalazi u Kristu kroz vršenje volje Očeve i ljubav.
magisterium.enrichment: Smisao života u katoličkom nauku neraskidivo je vezan uz nasljedovanje Krista, što se očituje u zapovijedi ljubavi koja sažima svu Božju volju. Čovjek se u potpunosti pronalazi samo kroz iskreni dar samoga sebe, što je temelj 'civilizacije ljubavi' koju Crkva promiče.
magisterium.assessment_en: Perfectly reflects the essence of the Christian message about the meaning of life which is found in Christ through doing the Father's will and love.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The meaning of life in Catholic teaching is inextricably linked to following Christ, which is manifested in the commandment of love that summarizes all of God's will. Man finds himself completely only through the sincere gift of himself, which is the foundation of 'the civilization of love' that the Church promotes.

sections.subtitle_en: Meaning of life: Will of the Father and love as the foundation of the Catholic message
sections.screenshot_description_en: Symbol of the cross or heart, representing the will of the Father and love as the meaning of life.
sections.content_en: To the question about the meaning of life, which is also posed by leaders in the field of artificial intelligence, Father Filip returns to the central message of the Catholic Church: **Jesus Christ**. He highlights two dominant aspects of Jesus' personality: doing the will of the Father and love for others. According to Father Filip, Jesus' actions were motivated by love and obedience to the Father, and imitating these principles would make the world a better place. He concludes that the beauty of the Catholic Church lies in the fact that, although it may not have all the answers, it offers content that satisfies the deepest human desires and questions. He quotes a parishioner who said: 'Jesus sells. Just give us Christ, and that's all you need', emphasizing that this message will be more needed than ever in the near future.

iterations.theme_en: Philosophical and theological perspectives of transhumanism, AI alignment, and the meaning of life
